framework_source: Government Digital and Data Profession Capability Framework (2026-03-23)

mappings.fde_description: Accessibility involves ensuring your service can be used by as many people as possible, including those with impaired vision, motor difficulties, cognitive impairments, learning disabilities and deafness.
mappings.ref_statement: Knowledge of data platform access management processes, including access request intake, provisioning workflows, access review cycles, and deprovisioning procedures.
mappings.notes: DDaT skill intersects with WIDAI DA domain. DA-K-016 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: RC-K-006 (RC, cosine: 0.311), OP-S-004 (OP, cosine: 0.290).

mappings.fde_description: Adapting to delivery methodologies involves contributing to discussions on the appropriate delivery methodology to use, and adapting your approach based on the delivery methodology of the team.
mappings.ref_statement: Translate research findings into production-applicable methods, working with engineering teams to operationalize novel approaches.
mappings.notes: DDaT skill intersects with WIDAI LS domain. LS-T-004 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: LS-T-003 (LS, cosine: 0.418), TF-A-004 (TF, cosine: 0.404).

mappings.fde_description: Agile and Lean practices involves encouraging teams to build incrementally, test and iterate their work based on regular feedback and other useful data.

mappings.notes: DDaT skill intersects with WIDAI DA domain. DA-T-011 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: LS-T-004 (LS, cosine: 0.372), LS-T-003 (LS, cosine: 0.364).

mappings.fde_description: Analysis involves examining, interpreting and analysing data to help make informed decisions.
mappings.ref_statement: Execute analytical requests from business stakeholders, including data extraction, transformation, analysis, and delivery of findings with documented methodology.
mappings.notes: DDaT skill superset of WIDAI AB domain. AB-T-001 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: AB-S-002 (AB, cosine: 0.547), AB-A-003 (AB, cosine: 0.532).

mappings.fde_description: Analysis and insight involves examining, interpreting and analysing data to help make informed decisions.
mappings.ref_statement: Ability to distinguish between correlation and causation in analytical findings, communicating the distinction and its implications for decision-making clearly to business audiences.
mappings.notes: DDaT skill superset of WIDAI AB domain. AB-A-003 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: AB-T-001 (AB, cosine: 0.526), AB-S-002 (AB, cosine: 0.524).

mappings.fde_description: Analysis and synthesis involves helping an organisation to adopt a wide range of analysis and synthesis techniques, and to continually assure, improve and innovate their practices to generate clear and valuable findings.
mappings.ref_statement: Translate research findings into production-applicable methods, working with engineering teams to operationalize novel approaches.
mappings.notes: DDaT skill intersects with WIDAI LS domain. LS-T-004 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: AB-S-003 (AB, cosine: 0.501), AB-T-001 (AB, cosine: 0.458).

mappings.fde_description: Analysis and synthesis (data ethics) involves expertly drawing together, analyse and evaluate qualitative and quantitative data; quickly read and interpret complex documents from a range of sources and distil to what is relevant; turning research data into clear findings that inform data ethics decisions for the entire organisation; build capacity in analysis and synthesis and involve others to increase consensus and challenge assumptions.
mappings.ref_statement: Execute analytical requests from business stakeholders, including data extraction, transformation, analysis, and delivery of findings with documented methodology.
mappings.notes: DDaT skill intersects with WIDAI AB domain. AB-T-001 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: DQ-K-013 (DQ, cosine: 0.495), DA-T-002 (DA, cosine: 0.473).

mappings.fde_description: Applied mathematics and statistics involves applying analytical methods including exploratory data analysis, visualisation and statistical testing to help make accurate recommendations.
mappings.ref_statement: Knowledge of descriptive statistics, including measures of central tendency, dispersion, distribution shape, and correlation, and how to interpret each in a business context.
mappings.notes: DDaT skill intersects with WIDAI AB domain. AB-K-001 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: AB-K-008 (AB, cosine: 0.459), AI-K-002 (AI, cosine: 0.459).

mappings.fde_description: Applied social sciences involves using an understanding of the social sciences to evaluate and challenge assumptions made in relevant projects.
mappings.ref_statement: Skill in designing and executing applied research projects with clear organizational relevance, balancing research rigor with practical applicability.
mappings.notes: DDaT skill intersects with WIDAI LS domain. LS-S-004 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: AB-S-007 (AB, cosine: 0.338), AB-S-002 (AB, cosine: 0.337).

mappings.fde_description: Applying statistical and analytical techniques involves using and evaluating methods to answer research questions and make data-informed recommendations.
mappings.ref_statement: Ability to select the appropriate analytical method for a given business question, balancing statistical rigor, time constraints, data availability, and audience comprehension capacity.
mappings.notes: DDaT skill superset of WIDAI AB domain. AB-A-002 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: AB-T-001 (AB, cosine: 0.544), AB-K-004 (AB, cosine: 0.528).

mappings.fde_description: Applying user-centred insights involves understanding all user needs and the problems that need to be solved, and applying insights to make decisions that meet these needs.
mappings.ref_statement: Knowledge of embedded analytics and augmented analytics concepts, including AI-assisted insight generation, natural language querying, automated anomaly detection, and their impact on self-service BI adoption.
mappings.notes: DDaT skill intersects with WIDAI AB domain. AB-K-010 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: AB-S-002 (AB, cosine: 0.430), TF-A-003 (TF, cosine: 0.419).

mappings.fde_description: Architect for the whole context involves assessing how trends in society and industry practices might impact the organisation; working with people outside of your organisation to inform policies, strategies and standards; anticipating changes to policy and build resilience through your architectural work; coaching others in identifying important trends.
mappings.ref_statement: Skill in designing ML training and serving infrastructure that scales from prototype to production without requiring architectural rework.
mappings.notes: DDaT skill intersects with WIDAI DA domain. DA-S-004 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: DQ-K-007 (DQ, cosine: 0.346), AG-S-002 (AG, cosine: 0.337).

mappings.fde_description: Communication involves conveying information using the most effective medium and language for the audience.
mappings.ref_statement: Ability to make defensible architectural decisions under uncertainty, documenting the rationale, rejected alternatives, and conditions that would trigger re-evaluation.
mappings.notes: DDaT skill intersects with WIDAI DA domain. DA-A-001 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: DG-S-001 (DG, cosine: 0.432), LS-S-010 (LS, cosine: 0.399).

mappings.fde_description: Asset and configuration management involves managing the life cycle of IT assets, such as hardware, software, intellectual property, licences and warranties, the configuration of components, and the relationships between them. Consideration is given to the usage, disposal, compliance, inventory, sustainability, cost optimisation and protection of the IT asset portfolio.
mappings.ref_statement: Manage development, staging, and production environments for data applications, including environment configuration, dependency specification, secrets management, and environment parity maintenance.
mappings.notes: DDaT skill intersects with WIDAI TF domain. TF-T-005 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: AB-T-007 (AB, cosine: 0.496), DQ-T-011 (DQ, cosine: 0.460).

mappings.fde_description: Availability and capacity management involves ensuring services are available with as little downtime or disruption as possible, and that we have sufficient resources to support emerging business needs.
mappings.ref_statement: Conduct capacity planning for data infrastructure, including growth projection analysis, resource utilization trending, and scaling recommendations aligned with business demand forecasts.
mappings.notes: DDaT skill intersects with WIDAI OP domain. OP-T-012 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: DQ-S-008 (DQ, cosine: 0.452), TF-T-007 (TF, cosine: 0.437).

mappings.fde_description: Business analysis involves understanding the business needs and translating those requirements into solutions through detailed analysis and feedback.
mappings.ref_statement: Execute analytical requests from business stakeholders, including data extraction, transformation, analysis, and delivery of findings with documented methodology.
mappings.notes: DDaT skill superset of WIDAI AB domain. AB-T-001 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: AB-S-010 (AB, cosine: 0.504), AB-T-002 (AB, cosine: 0.479).

mappings.fde_description: Business architecture involves using industry standards to develop integrated views of an organisation that help implement organisational strategy and solve complex problems.
mappings.ref_statement: Design and document the enterprise data architecture, including platform topology, data domain structure, integration patterns, and data lifecycle policies.
mappings.notes: DDaT skill intersects with WIDAI DA domain. DA-T-001 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: DA-T-002 (DA, cosine: 0.479), DA-S-001 (DA, cosine: 0.476).

mappings.fde_description: Business modelling involves visualising, analysing and communicating current and future business situations and how business components influence each other.
mappings.ref_statement: Skill in communicating model findings and limitations to non-technical business stakeholders in terms of business impact rather than technical metrics.
mappings.notes: DDaT skill intersects with WIDAI AI domain. AI-S-004 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: AB-S-005 (AB, cosine: 0.495), RM-K-011 (RM, cosine: 0.484).

mappings.fde_description: Business process improvement involves creating appropriate artefacts and analysing, modifying and testing existing and new processes to make them more efficient.
mappings.ref_statement: Skill in conducting operational process improvement for data workflows, identifying inefficiencies, designing automation opportunities, and measuring improvement outcomes.
mappings.notes: DDaT skill superset of WIDAI OP domain. OP-S-008 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: OP-A-002 (OP, cosine: 0.536), DQ-T-005 (DQ, cosine: 0.419).

mappings.fde_description:
You can:
- guide the organisation to ensure it has the specialist digital, data and technology skills it needs
- develop organisational processes and ways of working so that digital and data roles you support can thrive
- continuously improve and optimise the organisational environment
Examples of leadership using this skill:
- prioritising capability needs that will have the most impact
- negotiating for longer-term investment in people by articulating the risks and benefits of different staffing strategies
- advocating for good practice in ways of working and supporting people to adopt this practice
- growing digital and data communities
mappings.ref_statement: Skill in designing data and AI training programs for diverse organizational audiences, from executives to operational staff, with role-appropriate content and delivery formats.
mappings.notes: DDaT skill superset of WIDAI OP domain. OP-S-002 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: DG-S-006 (DG, cosine: 0.558), DG-S-003 (DG, cosine: 0.557).

mappings.fde_description: Change management involves managing changes to services, organisations, suppliers or configuration items, and the associated documentation.
mappings.ref_statement: Knowledge of organizational change management principles as applied to large-scale data and AI transformation programs, including adoption barriers, culture change levers, and executive sponsorship requirements.
mappings.notes: DDaT skill superset of WIDAI LS domain. LS-K-002 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: OP-K-003 (OP, cosine: 0.571), DQ-K-009 (DQ, cosine: 0.520).

mappings.fde_description:
You can:
- put in place continuous cyber and information security awareness and education programs, for example, training for specific groups
- use a range of methods to assess whether people’s security behaviour is improving
- compare the maturity of security awareness and culture in your organisation against others across government to determine effective improvements
Examples of leadership using this skill:
- incentivising the right behaviours across the whole organisation so that everyone knows how they contribute to security
- persuading senior leadership to represent a campaign personally
- communicating insights from previous incidents to ensure they are learned from
mappings.ref_statement: Skill in conducting and reviewing Data Protection Impact Assessments, identifying processing activities that present high risk to individuals, and recommending proportionate technical and organizational measures to mitigate identified risks.
mappings.notes: DDaT skill intersects with WIDAI SP domain. SP-S-003 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: LS-T-003 (LS, cosine: 0.451), RM-S-002 (RM, cosine: 0.449).

mappings.fde_description: Coding and scripting involves designing, writing and iterating code and scripts from prototype to production.
mappings.ref_statement: Skill in writing shell scripts to automate data operations, including file manipulation, job scheduling, environment configuration, log processing, and pipeline orchestration on Linux systems.
mappings.notes: DDaT skill intersects with WIDAI TF domain. TF-S-008 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: AI-S-008 (AI, cosine: 0.421), AI-S-003 (AI, cosine: 0.421).

mappings.fde_description: Commercial management involves exploring commercial opportunities whilst complying with the regulations on how we conduct and manage both internal and third party relationships.
mappings.ref_statement: Knowledge of product management practices as applied to data products, including roadmap prioritization, consumer discovery, feedback loops, and value measurement.
mappings.notes: DDaT skill intersects with WIDAI DQ domain. DQ-K-016 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: LS-S-013 (LS, cosine: 0.376), DQ-T-011 (DQ, cosine: 0.358).

mappings.fde_description: Commercial management involves exploring commercial opportunities whilst complying with the regulations on how we conduct and manage both internal and third party relationships.
mappings.ref_statement: Skill in negotiating strategic vendor partnerships for data and AI platforms, including commercial terms, roadmap alignment, dependency management, and exit strategy planning.
mappings.notes: DDaT skill intersects with WIDAI LS domain. LS-S-013 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: AI-S-004 (AI, cosine: 0.387), AB-A-001 (AB, cosine: 0.386).

mappings.fde_description: Communicating analysis and insight involves communicating relevant, compelling stories using the most appropriate medium; making complex recommendations at a tactical and strategic level.
mappings.ref_statement: Ability to identify and communicate when analytical findings challenge prevailing business narratives, presenting disconfirming evidence constructively and with appropriate context.
mappings.notes: DDaT skill superset of WIDAI AB domain. AB-A-001 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: AB-S-002 (AB, cosine: 0.583), LS-S-005 (LS, cosine: 0.579).

mappings.fde_description: Communicating between the technical and non-technical involves conveying information using the most effective medium and language for the audience.
mappings.ref_statement: Skill in communicating research findings to both technical and non-technical audiences, including paper writing, conference presentation, and internal briefing formats.
mappings.notes: DDaT skill superset of WIDAI LS domain. LS-S-005 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: OP-S-004 (OP, cosine: 0.558), AI-S-004 (AI, cosine: 0.513).

mappings.fde_description: Communicating data involves turning complex data into clear and well understood solutions, which can be acted upon; sharing data communication skills with the team and organisation; understanding and communicate different options, taking into account risks and uncertainties.
mappings.ref_statement: Skill in communicating data quality and data trust issues to non-technical executive stakeholders in terms of business risk and operational impact.
mappings.notes: DDaT skill superset of WIDAI DQ domain. DQ-S-009 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: OP-K-005 (OP, cosine: 0.531), OP-S-009 (OP, cosine: 0.507).

mappings.ref_statement: Ability to identify and resolve conflicts between data monetization objectives and data protection, privacy, and ethical obligations.

mappings.fde_description: Communication involves conveying information using the most effective medium and language for the audience.
mappings.ref_statement: Skill in communicating data and AI strategy, risk posture, and investment priorities to board-level and C-suite audiences using financial and business terms rather than technical language.
mappings.notes: DDaT skill intersects with WIDAI DG domain. DG-S-001 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: SP-A-003 (SP, cosine: 0.470), RC-A-003 (RC, cosine: 0.426).

mappings.fde_description: Collaboration in the Government Digital and Data profession typically involves working in a multidisciplinary project team, and contributing to working groups and wider professional communities. It requires a broad understanding of the technologies, principles and perspectives of related professions.
mappings.ref_statement: Ability to build cross-functional operational partnerships between data teams, IT infrastructure, business units, and vendor organizations to maintain effective data service delivery.
mappings.notes: DDaT skill superset of WIDAI OP domain. OP-A-003 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: DG-T-012 (DG, cosine: 0.556), OP-S-010 (OP, cosine: 0.549).

mappings.fde_description: Consultancy involves providing specialist advice to address stakeholder and business needs.
mappings.ref_statement: Execute analytical requests from business stakeholders, including data extraction, transformation, analysis, and delivery of findings with documented methodology.
mappings.notes: DDaT skill intersects with WIDAI AB domain. AB-T-001 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: LS-S-013 (LS, cosine: 0.412), SP-S-004 (SP, cosine: 0.400).

mappings.fde_description: Prototyping a service or product involves exploring, testing and sharing different concepts before committing to the final design.
mappings.ref_statement: Skill in designing ML training and serving infrastructure that scales from prototype to production without requiring architectural rework.
mappings.notes: DDaT skill intersects with WIDAI DA domain. DA-S-004 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: LS-K-005 (LS, cosine: 0.450), AI-S-019 (AI, cosine: 0.441).

mappings.fde_description: Context, problem and option analysis involves holistically investigating the context for a piece of work to define the problem or opportunity, understand root causes, identify high level business and user needs and shape the work to address those needs.
mappings.ref_statement: Skill in designing and executing complex analytical projects independently, including scoping, methodology selection, execution, and executive-level communication of findings.
mappings.notes: DDaT skill intersects with WIDAI AB domain. AB-S-003 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: AB-T-009 (AB, cosine: 0.375), AB-T-010 (AB, cosine: 0.370).

mappings.fde_description: Continual service improvement involves analysing current processes, and identify and implement opportunities to optimise them; leading and develop a team of experts to deliver service improvements; helping to evaluate and establish requirements for the implementation of changes by setting policy and standards.
mappings.ref_statement: Knowledge of IT service management principles as applied to data services, including service catalog design, service level management, incident and problem management, and continual service improvement.
mappings.notes: DDaT skill intersects with WIDAI OP domain. OP-K-007 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: OP-A-002 (OP, cosine: 0.460), TF-A-004 (TF, cosine: 0.377).

mappings.fde_description: Continuity management involves clearly defining processes and remediation plans to ensure the availability of systems and services in the event of unplanned downtime.
mappings.ref_statement: Manage the consent lifecycle across organizational systems, including consent collection configuration, preference storage, withdrawal processing, re-consent campaigns, and audit record maintenance.
mappings.notes: DDaT skill intersects with WIDAI RC domain. RC-T-013 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: DQ-T-011 (DQ, cosine: 0.447), RC-T-005 (RC, cosine: 0.432).

mappings.fde_description: Creating value for money involves making the right choices for the right reasons, balancing return on investment and value for the user and the organisation.
mappings.ref_statement: Ability to navigate the tension between data sharing for organizational value creation and data protection requirements, finding practical approaches that satisfy both objectives.
mappings.notes: DDaT skill intersects with WIDAI SP domain. SP-A-004 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: LS-K-007 (LS, cosine: 0.396), LS-K-001 (LS, cosine: 0.369).

mappings.fde_description: Customer service management involves managing customer service functions, such as responding to issue reports, information and access requests.
mappings.ref_statement: Knowledge of IT service management principles as applied to data services, including service catalog design, service level management, incident and problem management, and continual service improvement.
mappings.notes: DDaT skill superset of WIDAI OP domain. OP-K-007 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: OP-T-011 (OP, cosine: 0.429), OP-S-009 (OP, cosine: 0.386).

mappings.fde_description:
You can:
- implement cyber incident management processes that reflect the level of risk accepted by the organisation
- ensure the organisation is prepared for and can effectively respond to incidents
- determine the impact of critical incidents on the organisation’s technology
- lead the response to critical incidents that affect the whole organisation
Examples of leadership using this skill:
- helping the incident management team build relationships with senior leaders
- leading simulation exercises with the board so they understand their role in incident response processes and the implications of potential incidents
- assessing what to communicate to the board, ministers or the whole organisation, and when to do it
mappings.ref_statement: Monitor and analyze regulatory developments, enforcement actions, and supervisory authority guidance relevant to organizational data and AI activities, producing impact assessments and recommended responses.
mappings.notes: DDaT skill intersects with WIDAI RC domain. RC-T-008 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: RM-S-006 (RM, cosine: 0.456), RM-T-007 (RM, cosine: 0.442).

mappings.fde_description:
You can:
- maintain a clear understanding of the cyber and information security risks across the whole organisation
- develop and implement risk assessment processes
- oversee the assessment of the organisation's partners and supply chain for potential threats or vulnerabilities
Examples of leadership using this skill:
- building a culture where teams have awareness of, and take responsibility for, risks and mitigating them
- advising programme owners and people running IT services about risks in a way that enables them to act on that advice
- communicating with senior leaders in a way that enables them to make decisions about accepting or mitigating risk
mappings.ref_statement: Skill in designing and maintaining a model risk governance framework, including policy development, role and responsibility definition, escalation protocols, exception management, and board-level model risk reporting.
mappings.notes: DDaT skill superset of WIDAI RM domain. RM-S-006 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: SP-S-003 (SP, cosine: 0.549), RM-K-012 (RM, cosine: 0.537).

mappings.fde_description:
You can:
- develop an effective cyber and information security governance framework that is integrated with overall organisational governance
- prioritise issues affecting the organisation’s ability to meet security policies and follow standards
- lead a continuous review process to ensure governance meets changing regulations, legislation and best practices
- mitigate risks caused by unavoidable exceptions to the governance framework
Examples of leadership using this skill:
- escalating risks to the highest level in the organisation when governance issues do not have defined owners
- persuading others to use their financial resources for changes needed to comply with the framework
- helping stakeholders be aware of the risks that are caused when an exception is agreed
mappings.ref_statement: Skill in designing and maintaining a model risk governance framework, including policy development, role and responsibility definition, escalation protocols, exception management, and board-level model risk reporting.
mappings.notes: DDaT skill superset of WIDAI RM domain. RM-S-006 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: DG-K-002 (DG, cosine: 0.558), DG-K-007 (DG, cosine: 0.534).

mappings.fde_description: Analysis and insight involves examining, interpreting and analysing data to help make informed decisions.
mappings.ref_statement: Execute analytical requests from business stakeholders, including data extraction, transformation, analysis, and delivery of findings with documented methodology.
mappings.notes: DDaT skill superset of WIDAI AB domain. AB-T-001 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: OP-K-005 (OP, cosine: 0.561), AI-S-002 (AI, cosine: 0.551).

mappings.fde_description:
You can:
- identify and document where your organisation's data comes from and how it is shared
- assess current data systems in the organisation and the technology they use
- determine the data architecture the organisation needs to achieve organisational objectives
Examples of leadership using this skill:
- building relationships with parts of the organisation that protect access to data
- working with all areas of the organisation to ensure the data architecture works for everyone and any change is accepted
- relating data architecture to strategic objectives or important user needs to gain investment
mappings.ref_statement: Design and document the enterprise data architecture, including platform topology, data domain structure, integration patterns, and data lifecycle policies.
mappings.notes: DDaT skill superset of WIDAI DA domain. DA-T-001 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: DA-K-003 (DA, cosine: 0.610), DG-K-001 (DG, cosine: 0.577).

mappings.fde_description: Data compliance and security involves ensuring data handling practices at all stages of the data life cycle meet legal, regulatory, technical, ethical and organisation requirements to protect the data, data subjects and organisation.
mappings.ref_statement: Knowledge of data protection regulatory requirements, including GDPR, CCPA, HIPAA, SOX data requirements, and sector-specific data regulations, and the specific data management obligations each creates.
mappings.notes: DDaT skill equal WIDAI SP domain. SP-K-014 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: SP-T-006 (SP, cosine: 0.652), RC-A-005 (RC, cosine: 0.640).

mappings.fde_description: Data development process involves implementing data pipelines, products, services or platforms while ensuring they are secure, scalable, sustainable, reliable and meeting required standards.
mappings.ref_statement: Design, build, and maintain data ingestion pipelines from source systems to the data platform, including schema mapping, transformation logic, and error handling.
mappings.notes: DDaT skill superset of WIDAI DA domain. DA-T-005 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: DA-T-011 (DA, cosine: 0.548), DA-S-009 (DA, cosine: 0.543).

mappings.fde_description: Data engineering involves ensuring the correct systems are available to enable the collection, analysis and extrapolation of data.
mappings.ref_statement: Review and approve data model designs produced by data engineers and analysts, ensuring conformance with enterprise standards and architectural principles.
mappings.notes: DDaT skill intersects with WIDAI DA domain. DA-T-002 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: DQ-K-014 (DQ, cosine: 0.540), DA-T-005 (DA, cosine: 0.539).

mappings.fde_description: Data ethics and privacy involves using data and emerging data techniques in compliance with ethical and legal obligations.
mappings.ref_statement: Ability to identify and resolve conflicts between data monetization objectives and data protection, privacy, and ethical obligations.
mappings.notes: DDaT skill equal WIDAI SP domain. SP-A-001 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: SP-T-005 (SP, cosine: 0.598), RC-K-006 (RC, cosine: 0.596).

mappings.fde_description: Data governance involves the set of standards, processes and policies that ensures your data is consistent and reliable throughout the data life cycle.
mappings.ref_statement: Knowledge of data governance program design, including operating model options, council structures, policy frameworks, stewardship network models, and escalation path architectures.
mappings.notes: DDaT skill equal WIDAI DG domain. DG-K-007 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: DG-A-002 (DG, cosine: 0.742), DG-K-001 (DG, cosine: 0.713).

mappings.fde_description: Data governance involves the set of standards, processes and policies that ensures your data is consistent and reliable throughout the data life cycle.
mappings.ref_statement: Knowledge of data governance program design, including operating model options, council structures, policy frameworks, stewardship network models, and escalation path architectures.
mappings.notes: DDaT skill equal WIDAI DG domain. DG-K-007 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: DG-K-001 (DG, cosine: 0.715), DG-A-002 (DG, cosine: 0.713).

mappings.fde_description:
You can:
- review the organisation’s roles, responsibilities, policies, standards and attitudes to data
- decide the work needed to improve data governance, taking into account effort, reward and risk
- guide decision making on the most appropriate methods of implementing governance
- put in place data governance processes that ensure compliance and minimise risk
Examples of leadership using this skill:
- working with senior stakeholders to agree the level of risk the organisation is willing to tolerate
- getting senior stakeholders to prioritise improving data governance despite competing or changing priorities
- building stakeholders' confidence in new owners of data assets, for example, through training plans
